candidates.evidence_quote: Just on comp, obviously really strong trans quarter to date.
obviously really strong trans quarter to date. What's your sense of what's driving the underlying acceleration broadly?
i think there was a little bit of talk in in january about some positive weather
I think we're seeing um maybe maybe a return to more normalcy in terms of seasonality patterns if you will
certainly a little bit of the strength in the first week or two was was the omicron lapping on a year over year
we do the marketing and that type of offer is really off-premise driven. And we do that on occasion.
I think you said the restaurant margin should be at that 15% level for the overall corporate.
I talked about operating margins. I think you had said you were looking back to 19 and you wanted to get them back to that 5.2% range
our goal with G&A is to continue to move it equal or better as a percentage of sales every year, right, until we get to that 6% level
depreciation would probably be pretty similar year over year
Pre-opening will be up a little bit year over year.

we're hopeful to return to kind of a normal pricing cadence in the summertime. We'll be looking at what the trends are between now and then. I think the key for us is we're going to try to keep up with inflation.
we do do research, qualitative research, so we're constantly monitoring what our guests are saying about our value and making sure that that score is consistent
top end of consumers really does feel pressure. We're likely to actually benefit some from that because they typically move down off of the steakhouses and the expensive independents

I had a quick follow-up on the commodity outlook and then one on development. What percent of your basket do you currently have locked for 2023 at this point? Just trying to get a sense of the visibility you have on the mid-single-digit guidance there.
We're about 60%, which is pretty close. I mean, this time of year, we might want to be closer to 65 or so.
The category that is still a little bit tricky, obviously, is ground beef, harder to get a long-term contract there.
Elevated construction costs has clearly been a theme across the industry in 2022, but are you seeing any green shoots there as you look ahead?
while we would say we are seeing increases in our capex, that they're really not outpacing significantly the volumes that we're achieving
We've seen great sales performance out of the new locations that we've opened
we wouldn't be moving forward with the 20 to 22 locations if we didn't feel we'd be hitting the returns
if you think about a North Italia mature location and the CapEx may have moved up from $3.5 to $4 million, well, the sales there are about $8 million
I mean, we need to see stability in staffing.
We need to see stability in supply chain, really before you're talking about unlocking material additional margin opportunities

notes: Chunk contains a single event: Q&A portion (part 2 of 2) of the 2023-02-22 earnings call transcript; no fiscal_kpis array present in this chunk.

Borderline names kept for recall, flag for downstream review: supply_chain (evidence gives no narrower detail), seasonality, weather, depreciation_expense (mentioned only as a flat year-over-year guidance component).

Skipped as vague/duplicative/instance-bound: consumer trends normalization (vague), 45th-anniversary brand PR (vague), off-premise industry promotional activity (duplicate of off_premise_promotions), price elasticity monitoring (covered by menu_pricing/guest_value_perception), South Florida hurricane construction demand (single instance), war in Europe as inflation cause (single instance), pre-pandemic utilization comment (duplicate of restaurant_traffic).
